question: Where is our data processed and stored? How do you comply with GDPR for international data transfers?
title: Data Residency, Cross-Border Transfers, and GDPR Compliance Mechanisms
bullets: List all data processing and storage locations (regions, cloud providers, data centers)
Explain data residency options and customer control over region selection
Describe GDPR transfer mechanisms (SCCs, TIAs, supplementary measures post-Schrems II)
Clarify conditions for cross-border transfers and how consent/controls are enforced
suggested_inputs: Data residency and region selection documentation
Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) and Transfer Impact Assessment (TIA)
Architecture diagrams showing regional data flows
Policies describing supplementary measures for international transfers

good.text: We specify all processing and storage locations (e.g., EU, US, APAC regions). Customers can select and restrict data residency. EU data remains in EU unless explicitly configured otherwise.
For GDPR compliance, we use Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) and have completed Transfer Impact Assessments (TIA). We document risks and mitigation measures for all international transfers.
We implement supplementary measures post-Schrems II, including encryption, access controls, and minimization of transferred data. These are documented and auditable.
No cross-border transfers occur without explicit customer consent. We provide regional processing options and enforce controls to prevent unintended transfers.

partial.text: We operate in multiple regions, but detailed mapping of data flows and storage locations is not fully documented.
We comply with GDPR requirements, though specific transfer mechanisms such as SCCs or TIAs are not clearly explained.
Data may be stored in regional data centers, but customers have limited control over exact residency or transfer conditions.
We apply security controls for data protection, but do not explicitly describe supplementary measures for international transfers.

bad.text: We rely on Privacy Shield certification for GDPR compliance.
Data is stored in the cloud without specifying regions or transfer mechanisms.
GDPR does not apply to us regardless of where data is processed.
We cannot clearly state where data is processed or stored.

follow_ups: Can you guarantee that EU data never leaves the EU under configured settings?
Provide your Standard Contractual Clauses and Transfer Impact Assessment documentation.
What supplementary measures have you implemented following Schrems II?
If subject to GDPR, can you provide a European data processing addendum?
Do you have an EU representative as required under GDPR Article 27?
